Transcrição Completa: 4 Make More Money Scale With AI - Jelani     [00:00]  "from the one and only Mr. July. Big round of applause." "Thank you." "What's going on? I see."   [00:10]  "Uh, so today I'm going to talk about how you can make more money and scale with AI in 2025 and beyond. Um, but before I do that, I just want to give you a brief timeline of where we're at today."   [00:28]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Make More Money & Scale With AI - 2025 and Beyond"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Título principal da apresentação   [00:32]  "So, have a show of hands, how many people use Chad GPT every single day? That's a good number. Good. That's great."   [00:42]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "ChatGPT Growth Timeline"   NÚMEROS:  150 million users em 2 meses   VISUAL:  Fastest growing consumer app ever   [00:48]  "So, Chadbt grew to 150 million users in two months, making it the fastest growing consumer app ever. Um, which just shows how valuable AI is, right?"   [01:05]  "And of course, there's some people who will say it uses too much water or, you know, uses too much energy, but the efficiency gains that you're going to get by using AI, I think, uh, is way more impactful than what those people are saying."   [01:25]  "So in 2023 and 2024, which was kind of where we're at, we started to use multiple prompts together, right? Everyone knows what prompting is, I'm assuming. Yes. Yeah. Okay."   [01:42]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "AI Integration Evolution"   NÚMEROS:  2023-2024 timeline   VISUAL:  AI integrado em múltiplas ferramentas (Google Docs, Gemini, Slides)   [01:48]  "Um then what happened is AI started to become integrated in other tools. So you'll start to see, you know, AI in Google Docs or Google uh Gemini um slides, right? Like AI is pretty much everywhere now. Meaning that we can use AI outside of chat GBT, right? like we can use it for our benefit and our gain." I just use my AI avatar and people can't tell the difference."   [03:52]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Turn Knowledge Into Assets"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Conceito central da apresentação   [03:58]  "Um, so you essentially can turn your knowledge into assets. Um, again, go check out that talk if you want to learn more about that, but I want to talk about how you can turn AI into something that will increase your enterprise value. And that I think is really powerful in today's age."   [04:28]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "It All Starts With Knowledge"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Foundation do processo   [04:32]  "So it all starts with knowledge. So, obviously Jeremy created the hammer them strategy, right? And it's an SOP that I'm sure everyone here has reviewed or I hope you did. Um, and it's really in detail, right? It's in depth."   [04:52]  "And this is something that if you don't do, you definitely should have some type of SOP for the things that you do. Not only because it's going to make your company run more smooth smoothly, but it will also just if you ever wanted to exit, for example, you have operations in place that you that the new owner can follow."   [05:18]  "But really, what you want to do is accumulate all of your information into an SOP just like Jeremy does with all his VSLs or all his SOPs like the VSSL SOP, the webinar SOP, and all the other ones."   [05:38]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Hammer Them SOP Components"   NÚMEROS:  Múltiplos componentes   VISUAL:  Lista de aspectos: Campaign Objectives, Target Audience, etc.   [05:42]  "within those SOPs, right? There are, and this will make more sense in a moment, but there are so many different aspects to the hammer them SOP, right? Like you have to set campaign objectives, you have to define target audience, so on and so forth. There's a lot of different things that you have to do."   [06:05]  "However, within that SOP, there are so many opportunities that you can capitalize on, which is why you want to really d uh why you really want to create these SOPs because you'll find information or things that you can turn into assets that you can turn into opportunities, right? So, it all starts there."   [06:32]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "How To Collect Knowledge Quickly"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Tools: Loom (AI SOP generator), Scribe, Palow, Guide   [06:38]  "So, how do you actually collect knowledge quickly? I don't know your your process, Jeremy, but um you could do something like Loom. Loom has an AI SOP generator that you can just record and then turn into an SOP. You can use uh Scribe Palow, you can use Guide."   [07:00]  "These are all tools I've tested and they're pretty good at what they do. They just transcribe your content and then turn it into an SOP, right? And that's the quickest way to create these SOPs and really collect your knowledge."   [07:18]  "And your knowledge, your internal knowledge is data. Like that is gold right now. You'll be able to utilize that not only for yourself internally, but you'll be able to use it externally as well, which I'll talk about in a moment."   [07:38]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Transform Into Prompts"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Próximo passo do processo   [07:42]  "Um so once you collect your data or like your knowledge you want to transform that into prompts."   [07:52]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Anatomy of a Prompt"   NÚMEROS:  4 componentes   VISUAL:  1. Goal, 2. Define Output, 3. Constraints, 4. Context   [07:58]  "So real quick um the anatomy of a prompt is it's pretty simple. You want to state a goal. You want to basically define the output that you want. You want to give it constraints. So things you don't want to happen."   [08:18]  "So for example I shared an IC like a whole list of words that I will I don't want chatbt or call to ever use because it sounds like AI, right? You want to say stuff like that and you want to give it context."   [08:38]  "It's very simple, but like I get on calls all the time, like consulting like AI consulting calls, and that's like the number one thing they ask me is like, 'How do I get AI to sound more humanlike?' And it's because you're not following a structure. You're probably just entering a sentence and expecting that it's going to, you know, have magic, right?"   [09:05]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "5-Page Prompt Example"   NÚMEROS:  5 páginas   VISUAL:  Screenshot de prompt extremamente detalhado para ad generator   [09:10]  "And what I mean by this is like this. This is a five-page prompt. Does anyone like has anyone created a five-page prompt before?" "You upload that"   [09:22]  "Yeah, I create a five-page prompt for this is ad generator actually. That sounds really human. So, it's five pages. I upload this into a cloud project. Now, I have an ad generator that I can use over and over again."   [09:42]  "Like, this is the level of detail that you should be looking to do when it comes to prompting, not necessarily just like a paragraph and a sentence and expecting it to output like, you know, something that you can start running."   [10:02]  "Um, you could I honestly someone posted uh they posted an ad in IC chat. I could tell it was written by AI just based off the words like and if you're doing that if they're doing that everyone else is doing that and then it's just like you the marketplace is going to know that AI wrote that just based off the words, right?"   [10:28]  "So you have to give you have to give a lot of context, a lot of examples, which is probably the number one thing I tell people to is provide examples. AI is really good at replicating things and if you're able to provide really good examples, it's going to the output's just going to be way uh better."   [10:52]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Hammer Them Prompt Example"   NÚMEROS:  10 items por seção   VISUAL:  Exemplo de prompt estruturado baseado no SOP   [10:58]  "Um, so for the hammer them, I took the knowledge that Jeremy gave us and I turned it into a prompt. So this is an example. This is not a super long prompt. Some prompts don't have to be like super long depending on what you're trying to do."   [11:18]  "But to be clear, this prompt starts with the context, right? So you can't really see it, so I'll kind of read it out to you. So basically, you're saying you're an expert in the hammer them ad strategy. Your goal is to generate comprehensive strategy based on the provided inputs. Okay? So provided inputs is the questions or your audience, your offer, uh stuff like that, right?"   [11:48]  "And then you must provide exactly 10 items for each section formatted as questions where appropriate. So I give it context immediately. I describe what the four pillars are that Jeremy describes. And by the way, you could turn other um aspects of the hammer them strategy into these like different props, right? I'm just focusing on the four pillars for now."   [12:18]  "Um, but the whole point is like if you have the SOP, you can really dive deep. Then you provide the SOP. So, um, in Claude, I actually did this before I turned it into what I'm about to show you. But I uploaded this, provided Jeremy's SOP, the prompt, context, requirements of what I want that output to be, and then I have really good questions that could be used for the hammer them, right? It just makes your life easier"   [12:52]  "once you have the knowledge collected, once you have the prompts, you can then turn those into AI tools that you own."   [13:02]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Turn Into AI Tools You Own"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Transformação de prompts em assets   [13:08]  "So it it then turns into an asset that you can use internally, but most likely you want to use it externally, meaning you want to use it as a lead generator or a revenue driver. And it's a it's a simple tool, but it's really impactful." It took me almost 1.5 months to actually get this going. I couldn't figure it out. This just made it so much easier." [15:38]  "And you might have heard the term like uh AI wrapper, right? This is essentially what it is, but it's it's your data. It's your information, right? And it allows you to create these assets that increase your enterprise value."   [15:58]  "And not to mention that these tools can turn into full-blown software if you wanted to. Like you can turn you you can turn these micro tools, as I like to call them, into full-blown software." Live streams, podcasts, white papers, no, it's tools." He has SEO analyzers, he has answer the public, he has AB testing calculator, Uber suggest, ads grader, mail grader, backlinks checker, AI writer like he has all these tools."   [19:42]  "A lot of them are free and he uses them to attract leads into his ecosystem, which I think is really interesting. And I'm pretty sure all of you guys who have offers can think about like what tools you could potentially create to attract leads and prospects into your ecosystem just like Neil Patel is doing."   [20:10]  "Um, so the whole goal really is to like think about these tools and how they play into your ecosystem or into your offer, into your business, and how you can capitalize on them by, you know, capturing your knowledge, turning it into a prompt, and turning it into a potential AI tool."   [20:32]  "And like Jeremy says, you should be leveraging AI any way, shape, or form, right? So the time is now to do that."   [20:42]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "What To Make - Ideas"   NÚMEROS:  Múltiplas categorias   VISUAL:  Lista: Automated ad creation, Dynamic content, SEO optimization, Chatbots, Knowledge bases, Content rewriters, Data analysis, Content ideation, Fulfillment, Lead acquisition   [20:48]  "So if you're thinking about what to make, right? Like these are some ideas. So you can do automated ad creation, uh dynamic content, SEO optimization, you can do chat bots, knowledge bases, content rewriters, which is a hot topic actually, uh data analysis, um content ideation, fulfillment, lead acquisition. There's a lot of things that you can do and these are just some ideas."   [21:20]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "How Amazon AWS Got Started"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Case study de transformação de cost center em profit center   [21:25]  "I'm curious to know though, do you guys know how Amazon AWS got started? No."   [21:35]  "So what happened was um they were spending a lot of money on their infrastructure and Jeff was like we should not be spending all this money. We should be doing it ourselves."   [21:52]  "So what he did was he turned his cost center into a profit center. He took the idea of like why are we paying some other company and spending all this money and we could just do it ourselves and then create an asset that we own."   [22:12]  "And now I think a AWS does like like I don't know the exact number so I'm not gonna make up one but it's it's a lot. Um, but you turn your call centers into profit centers, right?"   [22:28]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Turn Automations Into Tools"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Zapier e Make automations como base para tools   [22:35]  "How do you do this? One idea, all your Zap year automations, all your make automations, those can be AI tools. And you might be thinking, well, why would I turn that into a tool if it's just like an automation?"   [22:52]  "Again, you have a front-facing user interface or something that can be utilized outside of just internal, you know, your internal operations. You can then sell this. You can turn this into a lead generation um mechanism, right?"   [23:15]  "There's a lot of things that you can do and I'm sure you guys have, you know, created these really complex or really awesome automations that can be used as tools for um for yourself or for your customers and clients."   [23:35]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "How To Build: Bolt.new, Lovable.dev, Cursor"   NÚMEROS:  2 dias para criar tool   VISUAL:  Interfaces de no-code/low-code AI tools   [23:42]  "So, how do you do this? So, there's AI tools right now that are similar to to uh chat GPT where you just talk to it in natural language. So, both new lovable.dev and cursor."   [24:02]  "So, I'm not a dev per se. Uh I'm I would say I'm techy but I'm not a dev. But with these interfaces I can take my ideas, use natural language and then create these assets."   [24:22]  "Like for example the hammer them strategy tool that I showed before. I just had a conversation with Bolt and I created this tool. Now I will say that there is some friction there. You will have to um you have to understand a little bit of like software development because there's authentication, there's like uh data pipelines and architecture and stuff like that."   [24:52]  "But the point is you can start to really conceptualize these ideas using AI to get those ideas in the forefront and just have conversations. And again, AI doesn't judge you. AI doesn't really care if you ask it a quote unquote dumb question. It's going to answer you with the best possible uh answer and you can just learn that way, which I think is really powerful."     📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO   NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS: ChatGPT: 150 million users em 2 meses (fastest growing consumer app ever) Neil Patel: $100M AR, tools = highest ROI 5-page prompts para ad generators 10 items por seção em prompts estruturados 1.5 meses economizados por usuários do Hammer Them tool 2 dias para criar content rewriter tool $50K-70K/year copywriter vs $10 AI agent   ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:   The Knowledge to Value Cycle: Collect Knowledge  → SOPs, Resources, Frameworks, Examples Transform Into Prompts  → Structured, detailed prompts Develop Tools  → AI wrappers using your data Deploy Internally/Externally  → Lead gen, revenue driver, value add-on Use AI Agents  → Autonomous tools using your frameworks Maximize Value  → Increased enterprise value   Anatomy of a Prompt (4 Components): State a Goal:  What you want to accomplish Define Output:  Specific format/structure expected Give Constraints:  Words/phrases to avoid, boundaries Provide Context:  Examples, frameworks, SOPs   Knowledge Collection Tools: Loom:  AI SOP generator (record → transcribe → SOP) Scribe:  Transcription to SOP Palow:  Documentation tool Guide:  SOP creation   Prompt Best Practices: Create 5-page detailed prompts (not paragraphs) Provide extensive context #1 Rule: Provide examples  (AI is great at replication) Upload list of words to avoid (AI-sounding language) Use structured format (Goal → Output → Constraints → Context) Include SOP content directly in prompt   AI Tool Building Platforms: Bolt.new:  Natural language to software Lovable.dev:  No-code AI tool builder Cursor:  AI-powered development Claude Projects:  For uploading long prompts/SOPs All use natural language (no dev experience required initially)   AI Avatar & Voice Tools: HeyGen:  AI avatar creation for content at scale Captions:  AI avatars for e-commerce Arcads:  E-commerce focused AI avatars 11 Labs:  Voice duplication/cloning Use case: Fix VSSL mistakes without re-recording Change hooks without studio time People can't tell difference   Tool Ecosystem Strategy: Start with ONE SOP → ONE tool Build multiple tools from different SOPs Create interconnected ecosystem Use tools as: Lead generators (like Neil Patel) Revenue drivers Value add-ons to offers Internal efficiency gains   Neil Patel Tool Strategy: Tools dropdown on main navigation SEO Analyzer Answer The Public AB Testing Calculator Ubersuggest Ads Grader Mail Grader Backlinks Checker AI Writer Most are FREE  (lead magnets) Highest ROI vs webinars/case studies/podcasts/white papers Best payback period vs cost   What Tools To Build (Ideas): Automated ad creation Dynamic content generators SEO optimization Chatbots (customer support) Knowledge bases Content rewriters Data analysis Content ideation Fulfillment automation Lead acquisition   Turn Cost Centers Into Profit Centers (AWS Model): Jeff Bezos: "Why pay others? Do it ourselves." Infrastructure costs → AWS (now massive profit center) Your Zapier/Make automations → AI tools you can sell Internal operations → External products Cost reduction → Revenue generation   Content Rewriter Example: Started as Make automation (internal) Built in 2 days using Bolt.new Sold to client as standalone tool Takes YouTube link → transforms content Value add-on to client's offer   Amazon Course Chatbot Example: Client has Amazon course Transcribed all course content Uploaded to custom chatbot system Students ask questions → instant answers Reduces customer support load Increases time to value for students   Hammer Them Tool Case Study: Took Jeremy's SOP Created structured prompt (4 pillars focus) Built tool in Claude/Bolt Posted in Inner Circle Immediate positive feedback: "Saved me when scrambling for client" "Took 1.5 months before, now instant" Jeremy now owns the asset Lead generator + value add   ESTRUTURA:   2023-2024 AI Evolution: Multiple prompts used together AI integrated into existing tools (Google suite) Cloud projects for complex workflows Shift from standalone to ecosystem   Why Create AI Tools: Increase Enterprise Value:  Tools are assets on balance sheet Lead Generation:  Free tools attract prospects (Neil Patel model) Revenue Driver:  Sell tools or add to offer Operational Efficiency:  Internal use reduces costs Competitive Advantage:  Teams with AI outperform those without Scalability:  Micro tools → full-blown software Exit Preparation:  Operations documented, transferable   The AI Wrapper Concept: Not just any AI wrapper YOUR data + YOUR frameworks + YOUR SOPs Unique to your business Defensible asset Increases valuation   Micro Tools → Full Software: Start small (single function) Validate with users Expand functionality Can hire dev to finish if needed Build ecosystem of interconnected tools   Reddit Answers Case Study: Beta release Chat interface All Reddit data uploaded Discoverable through conversation Similar to ChatGPT experience Big companies doing what you can do   Key Mental Shifts: Internal knowledge = Gold:  Document everything SOPs = Opportunities:  Each SOP = potential tool Tools > Content:  Highest ROI according to Neil Patel Cost Centers → Profit Centers:  AWS model Automation → Product:  Zapier flows = sellable tools AI doesn't judge:  Learn by asking "dumb" questions   Common Mistakes: One-sentence prompts:  Not enough context No examples provided:  AI needs patterns to replicate Using AI-sounding words:  Marketplace recognizes it Not collecting knowledge:  Can't build tools without SOPs Thinking you need to be a dev:  Natural language tools exist   Building Process: Create detailed SOP (or use existing) Identify opportunities within SOP Build 5-page prompt with examples Test in Claude/ChatGPT Use Bolt.new/Lovable/Cursor to build interface Launch internally first Gather feedback Launch externally Iterate and expand   Where To Start: Tasks that cost money:  Automate expensive processes Processes taking most time:  Efficiency gains Roles hardest to hire/train:  Replace with AI agents Customer support questions:  Build knowledge base chatbot Content creation:  Automate with your frameworks   Value Proposition: Don't hire $50-70K/year copywriter Pay $10 for AI agent using your frameworks Non-stop work (24/7) No raises, taxes, emotional trauma Gets better and cheaper over time 10x smarter every year   DADOS FINANCEIROS:   Cost Comparisons: Traditional employee: $50K-70K/year (copywriter example) AI agent: $10 (one-time or minimal recurring) Savings: $50K-70K/year per role automated   ROI Data (Neil Patel): $100M annual revenue Tools = highest ROI of all content types Beat: Webinars, Case studies, Live streams, Podcasts, White papers Best payback period vs cost   Tool Development Costs: Content rewriter: 2 days to build (using Bolt.new) Hammer Them tool: Time not specified but implied quick Amazon chatbot: Time not specified Generally: Days not months with AI-powered tools   Enterprise Value Impact: Tools = assets on balance sheet Increases valuation for exit Documented operations = transferable value Reduces human capital dependency Scales without proportional cost increase   Efficiency Gains: ChatGPT: 150M users in 2 months (unprecedented growth) 1.5 months of work → instant (Hammer Them feedback) Customer support → automated (chatbot) Content creation → scaled (AI avatars) Voice recording → never redo (11 Labs)   AWS Analogy: Infrastructure cost center → profit center Now: Massive revenue (exact number not disclosed but "a lot") Model: Internal tool → external product   Zapier/Make Automation Value: Existing automations = sunk cost Turn into products = new revenue stream Front-end UI = sellable asset Internal efficiency → external monetization   AI Agent Economics: Get 10x smarter each year Get cheaper each year (exponential improvement) Eventually: Dollars for hyper-productive work No raises, taxes, benefits, PTO 24/7 operation   Time Investment (Jelani's Content): 3 hours/week recording 30 minutes/day posting/producing Highest ROI activity Lifetime asset creation   Free Consultation Offer: 30-minute AI consult (free) QR code provided Partner: Ted from Agentesk AI Focus: AI tools and ideas for specific offers   DURAÇÃO:  24:54  FORMATO:  Apresentação presencial em mastermind sobre AI tools e scaling
